name: HAO TRAN - Inventor, Founder and CEO
about: Mr. Tran Duy Hao is a highly accomplished inventor and entrepreneur with over 20 years of international experience across diverse industries, including Aerospace engineering, Oil & Gas, and Digital Transformation, having successfully led international projects in more than 20 countries across 4 continents. As the hands-on Founder & CEO of StarGlobal 3D, he has pioneered the development of 3D/360 smart interactive mapping systems, which have been granted exclusive patents by the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) in 2021 and the National Office of Intellectual Property of Vietnam (NOIP) in 2024. In recognition of his significant contributions to science and technology, Tran Duy Hao was conferred an Honorary Doctorate of Philosophy in Science and Technology by the International American University in August 2024.

recommendations: ["Lance S. “To Whom It May Concern: This to confirm Mr. Tran Duy Hao has worked under my supervision as a Drilling Engineer based in Bangkok while I was previously employed with Transocean both in Vietnam and mostly in Thailand. Mr. Tran Duy Hao is Vietnamese and based out of Ho Chi Minh City. He is married with two children and is very good/devoted family man. He is currently looking for other employment which will give better opportunity to better provide for his family and has a strong motivation accordingly. While Mr. Tran Duy Hao worked under my supervision for Transocean rig operations in Thailand, I found him to be hard working, technically very competent as a Drilling Engineer and the personality to get along with people, but at the same time get his tasks accomplished. Since leaving Transocean, Mr. Tran Duy Hao has been employed by two other companies: Being involved the shipyard project of a new build Jack up with Vantage Drilling, then start-up operations of that new jack up offshore West Africa. However, he had to resign from Vantage due to family relocation issue to Ivory Coast caused by his new-born baby vaccination at that time; And started working for PV Drilling upon return back to Vietnam, having attended and completed a new build project of a semi-submersible tender rig giving him a wealth of experience in project management. While employed with Transocean, he gained a lot of experience in setting up a jack-up drilling rig for offline activities such as tubular stand building while drilling, cementing and logging. He also has the experience for operational excellent both in HSE&T and performance in drilling. Prior to going into the oilfield, Mr. Tran Duy Hao achieved a Bachelor of Engineering in Aeronautical Engineering from the University of Technology in Ho Chi Minh City Vietnam which lends well with his current ambitions working as a Drilling Engineer. He also has completed a variety of courses associated with the oilfield per his CV. In summary I have no reservation to recommend Mr. Tran Duy Hao to be employed in a Drilling Engineering or Project Management position.”"]

patents: [{"date_issued":"September 5, 2024","description":"Một hệ thống quàn lý thông minh trên nền tảng bản đồ số hóa 3D/3600 tích hợp đầy đủ thông tin theo thời gian thực cỏ khả năng hiển thị các thuộc tính theo thời gian thực và điều khiển các thiết bị kết nổi mạng trong khu vực địa lý được số hóa, bao gồm các bước: tạo lập bản đồ số 3D/360 cùa khư vực địa lý này sừ dụng ít nliât một thiết bị hình ảnh; kết xuất các thuộc tính thời gian thực thành dữ liệu hình ảnh cỏ thể hiển thị; hiển thị các thuộc tính thời gian thực tại một điểm quan sát cụ thể khi người dùng truy vấn dữ liệu tại điểm quan sát hoặc khi người dùng quét mă vạch, mã QR đại diện cho đối tượng đó; điều khiển thiết bị kết nối mạng ĩoT bằng thao tác trên giao diện màn hình hoặc bằng giọng nói ở chế độ rảnh tay.","patents_id":"1-2018-04873","title":"Hệ thống quản lý thông minh bằng bản đồ số hóa ba chiều/360 độ (3D/360°) tích hợp đầy đủ thông tin theo thời gian thực và phương pháp tạo ra hệ thống này"},{"date_issued":"February 16, 2021","description":"A smart management integrated mapping system (SMIMS) having the capability of displaying real-time properties and controlling network-based devices in a geographical area is disclosed which comprises: generating a 3D/360° map of the geographical area using at least one imaging devices; about: TYPIC TRAVEL CO., LTD est une Agence Réceptive Locale, basée à Hanoi - Vietnam, qui s'occupe des Organisations des Voyages au Vietnam, Laos, Cambodge, Birmanie et Thailande. Sa mission est de vous aider à construire votre Voyage 100% sur mesure, en mêlant authenticité et originalité, proposant un guide privé sur les destinations Vietnam Laos Cambodge et Birmanie. name: Nha Thanh Nguyen
about: Thanh Nha Nguyen is the Executive Director of the Vietnam–United States Fall Forum (VNxUS), the premier annual bilateral forum connecting Vietnam and the United States. The forum convenes government leaders, executives, startup founders, and experts from healthcare, education, and local governance sectors. Hosted across key U.S. cities, VNxUS facilitates strategic dialogues and partnerships in trade, investment, technology, and economic diplomacy. Alongside his leadership at VNxUS, Nha is a Visiting Research Fellow at HEC Montréal, where his research interests span innovation, sustainable development, international regulatory frameworks, and human development. He also serves as a Research Assistant at HEC Montréal’s MOSAIC research center, contributing to interdisciplinary projects that bridge academic insights with practical innovation and policy implementation. With over 20 years of experience, Nha is a recognized thought leader in sustainable development, social entrepreneurship, and economic governance. His extensive experience includes consulting for the World Bank (WB) and the Asian Development Bank (ADB), along with senior leadership roles in international NGOs. Beyond economic and social innovation, Nha maintains a deep interest in cognitive science and mindfulness, exploring how these fields intersect with leadership effectiveness, decision-making, and human resilience. His integrated approach to mindfulness and cognitive development enriches his work in leadership training and policy design. Nha holds prestigious fellowships, including the Swedish Institute Global Executive Programme (2024) and Acumen Academy Social Impact Leadership (2023), through which he brings global perspectives on leadership, governance, and sustainable economic transformation.

publications: [{"date":"June 10, 2025","description":"In his keynote speech at the East-West Center on June 10, 2025, Thanh Nha Nguyen, Executive Director of the Vietnam–United States Fall Forum (VNxUS), provided insightful analysis and perspectives on the evolving economic relationship between Vietnam and the United States, focusing on global value chains amidst ongoing tariff uncertainty. Highlighting the historical milestone of 30 years of normalized diplomatic ties and the 2023 elevation to a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, Nguyen emphasized Vietnam’s rapid integration into global manufacturing sectors such as electronics, textiles, and renewable energy.Nha Nguyen outlined how critical U.S. policies—the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), the CHIPS and Science Act, and the Indo-Pacific Economic Framework (IPEF)—are reshaping trade dynamics and creating new opportunities for Vietnam. While acknowledging current uncertainties caused by recent U.S. tariff proposals initiated by President Trump, he stressed Vietnam’s strategic responses, marked by diplomatic engagement and proactive policy adjustments.Significantly, Nha Nguyen pointed to promising avenues for bilateral collaboration, specifically in semiconductor manufacturing, clean energy solutions, rare-earth mineral extraction, and digital innovation. He advocated strongly for Vietnam’s shift from a mere production base to a full-fledged partner in high-tech and innovation-driven industries, underscoring the importance of upskilling the local workforce and fostering a thriving, inclusive economic environment.Concluding with a forward-looking vision, Nha Nguyen highlighted that despite temporary challenges, the U.S.–Vietnam partnership holds vast potential for mutual prosperity and regional stability. He passionately called for deeper institutional cooperation, transparency, and inclusive growth strategies that could empower SMEs, startups, and rural communities, ensuring the benefits of globalization and innovation are shared widely and sustainably.","subtitle":"East-West Center","title":"U.S.–Vietnam Economic Relations and Global Value Chains: Navigating Uncertainty and Building Inclusive Innovation"},{"date":"July 6, 2024","description":"Nha Nguyen, a visionary leader in sustainable development and education, delivered an inspiring keynote address titled \"AI and Human Sustainability\" at the TraceVerified Carbon Credit Internship Training Programme Opening. Reflecting on 20 years of transformative experiences, he shared his journey from the Central Highlands of Vietnam to his global academic and entrepreneurial pursuits.In his speech, Nha Nguyen highlighted the revolutionary role of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in advancing sustainability. He discussed how AI can address complex challenges like climate change by optimising energy usage, modelling environmental risks, and fostering renewable energy solutions. However, he also emphasised the irreplaceable human qualities of mindfulness, intuition, and emotional intelligence—key traits that set humans apart from machines.Nha Nguyen commended the training programme for equipping participants with critical skills in areas such as carbon credit accounting, project evaluation, and strategic policymaking for the green economy. He encouraged attendees to blend technology with human insight to create innovative and impactful solutions for a sustainable future.Concluding with Steve Jobs' timeless advice—\"Stay hungry, stay foolish\"—he left the audience inspired to approach their careers with purpose and determination, reminding them that meaningful change begins with mindful and heartfelt actions.","subtitle":"TraceVerified","title":"Giving Keynote Speech at Carbon Credit Intership Training Program Opening"},{"date":"March 25, 2024","description":"On Vietnam Social Work Day, Nha Nguyen delivered an inspiring address celebrating the spirit of empathy and collective action in social work. Reflecting on over 35 years of impact by the Christina Noble Children’s Foundation (CNCF), he highlighted its transformative work in education, healthcare, and community development, which has touched over a million lives. Guided by the belief of founder Christina Noble that \"the essence of human nature is to feel the pain of others as our own and to act to alleviate that pain,\" Nha Nguyen emphasised the profound role of social work as a mission of compassion and innovation. He called on attendees to reaffirm their commitment to creating a more sustainable and compassionate future through collaboration and action.","subtitle":"University of Social Sciences and Humanities - Vietnam National University Ho Chi Minh CIty","title":"Vietnam Social Work Day at University of Social Sciences and Humanities"},{"date":"November 11, 2023","description":"On November 11, 2023, Nha Nguyen was honoured to speak at the “Meeting with Heads of Diplomatic Missions and Foreign Organisations in Vietnam” in Binh Dinh Province. The event, attended by over 150 global representatives, highlighted the strength of international collaboration. Nha Nguyen shared insights into key development programmes in education, health, nutrition, and community development, which have supported vulnerable children and families. The conference also explored new partnership opportunities in Binh Dinh, reaffirming a collective commitment to sustainable social change.","subtitle":"Binh Dinh People's Committee","title":"Meeting with Heads of Diplomatic Missions and Foreign Organisations in Vietnam"},{"date":"July 16, 2009","description":"Co-authored by Nha Nguyen, this article examines how greater central bank independence could enhance Vietnam's economic stability. It highlights that while higher independence often correlates with lower inflation and reduced deficits, its impact on growth is less certain. The authors argue that Vietnam’s government-influenced central bank should embrace more autonomy to better achieve fiscal and monetary goals.","subtitle":"the Saigon Economics Times","title":"Central Bank Independence: A Pragmatic Insight for Vietnam's Economic Resilience"}]
projects: [{"description": "The Sunshine Judo Centre, an initiative by the Christina Noble Children\u2019s Foundation (CNCF), was inaugurated last Thursday, marking a significant addition to CNCF's enduring 35-year legacy alongside the Sunshine School and Sunshine Homes. This pioneering sports centre in Vietnam is dedicated to empowering underprivileged children by providing access to judo and educational opportunities, aiming to foster physical, emotional, and intellectual growth.The event was graced by distinguished figures from various sectors, including local government officials from MOLISA and the People\u2019s Committee of District 10, representatives from international judo clubs, the Olympic Vietnam Committee, and corporate leaders from prominent companies like Standard Chartered Bank and BITIS.Designed not merely as a sports facility, the centre serves as a holistic development hub for approximately 200 children, including those living with disabilities or facing economic hardships. These children now have access to professional judo training alongside a range of educational programs that promote values such as resilience, teamwork, and discipline.The inauguration also coincided with the launch of the Noble Summer Camp and CNCF\u2019s Children's Olympics, highlighting CNCF\u2019s commitment to youth empowerment through sports. These programs are aimed at instilling sportsmanship and enhancing physical and mental well-being, providing a nurturing and inclusive environment where every child can thrive and achieve their potential.The Sunshine Judo Centre stands as a beacon of hope and opportunity, embodying CNCF\u2019s mission to provide every child the right to a fulfilling life, free from hardship and filled with opportunities to succeed.", "start_date": "Apr 2024", "title": "Sunshine Judo Centre"}, {"description": "SIC Mart is a groundbreaking social business aimed at narrowing the gaps in quality education, career development, and job opportunities for disadvantaged groups in Vietnam. The initiative targets individuals facing societal challenges, including persons with disabilities, LGBTQ+ communities, ethnic minorities, and those in extreme poverty. Through skills training, job placement, and facilitating ESG and DEI practices in businesses, SIC Mart addresses root causes like discrimination, lack of social inclusion, and inadequate legal frameworks.The project has achieved significant milestones, including:Empowering over 1,800 individuals through training and job placement.Building a community of 30+ social impact businesses.Promoting social inclusion and driving UN SDG goals in Vietnam.SIC Mart proudly reached the Grand Final of the Social Business Creation 2024 competition and is recognised as one of the top 10 teams globally for its innovative and sustainable approach to social challenges.", "start_date": "2024", "title": "SIC Mart (Social Impact Creation Mart)"}, {"description": "Gravity Water and the Christina Noble Children\u2019s Foundation (CNCF) have partnered on innovative projects to enhance water security and climate resilience in Vietnamese schools. These initiatives focus on integrating advanced rainwater harvesting and filtration technologies to provide sustainable water solutions.Project Overview:Mekong Delta Initiative: Targets the installation of rainwater systems in 50 schools in Ben Tre Province in 2024, aiming to expand to 500 schools across the Mekong Delta over five years.Ho Chi Minh City Project: Involves equipping 85 schools in Ho Chi Minh City with similar systems, serving as a model for effective public-private collaboration.Key Features:Rainwater Harvesting: Implements cutting-edge technology to supplement traditional water supplies with rainwater, reducing dependence on groundwater.Water Quality Improvement: Ensures safe water through multi-stage treatments including sediment filtration, carbon treatment, and UV sterilization.Infrastructure Optimization: Enhances existing water systems with IoT technology to minimize water loss and maximize efficiency.Educational Component: Provides tools and programs to educate students on water conservation and STEM applications in environmental management.Impact and Evaluation:These projects aim to reduce water scarcity impacts, enhance student health, and offer educational benefits by ensuring access to clean water. Funded entirely by Gravity Water, the initiatives are designed to be scalable and serve as benchmarks for similar programs globally.By merging Gravity Water\u2019s technology with CNCF\u2019s community focus, these initiatives not only address immediate water needs but also foster long-term sustainability and resilience in Vietnam\u2019s educational sector.", "start_date": "Nov 2023", "title": "School Rainwater Initiative - Mekong Delta and Ho Chi Minh City"}, {"description": "The Christina Noble Education Programme (CNEP) is a multi-faceted initiative engineered to break down barriers to quality education for underprivileged youth in Vietnam. The programme champions professional development, life skills, and academic support through four pivotal elements: Christina Noble Scholarships, Christina Noble Fellowship, Christina Noble Internship, and Christina Noble Mentorship.Scholarships provide vital financial aid, ensuring that economic constraints don't inhibit talent. The Fellowship programme, running from November to January, employs project-based learning to hone practical skills essential for career success. The Internship bridges academia and professional life, providing real-world work experience, while Mentorship offers invaluable guidance from seasoned professionals.Strategically partnered with universities and training bodies, CNEP also integrates key competencies like entrepreneurial thinking, effective communication, and mindfulness into its curriculum. Through methodical assessment and stringent eligibility criteria, the programme maintains an unwavering commitment to excellence, mirroring CNCF's long-standing mission to empower communities.CNEP not only prepares participants for professional life but fosters long-term connections and networks, augmenting their career paths. By nurturing multi-dimensional growth, CNEP aims to groom the next generation of leaders, fortified with the skills and mindset to drive positive societal change.", "start_date": "Oct 2023", "title": "Christina Noble Education Programme"}, {"start_date": "Aug 2023", "title": "Children Walk For Hope"}, {"description": "The School Meal Programme, created by Nha Nguyen in June 2023, is a transformative initiative that addresses critical challenges faced by disadvantaged preschool and primary school children in highland mountainous regions, particularly those from ethnic minority backgrounds. For many children, the programme is life-saving, as it ensures access to nutritious meals they might otherwise miss, preventing them from staying home due to a lack of resources for lunch. By providing free, healthy meals at school, the programme promotes regular attendance, enhances physical and cognitive development, and creates opportunities for a better future.For parents, the benefits extend beyond peace of mind. They no longer need to take time out of their day to bring meals to school or pick their children up to eat at home, saving valuable time and energy. This allows parents to focus on work or other responsibilities, empowering them to secure stable jobs and improve their livelihoods. In this way, the programme supports not only children\u2019s growth but also the well-being and productivity of entire families.", "start_date": "Jun 2023", "title": "School Meal Programme"}, {"description": "Fostering Resilience and Joy in Disadvantaged CommunitiesBackgroundThe Christina Noble Children's Foundation recognises the necessity of going beyond material support to create lasting impact. In a pursuit to holistically develop individuals and communities, CNCF has initiated the Noble Academy of Wellbeing and Happiness.ObjectivesThe programme aims to:Equip disadvantaged children and families with emotional intelligence skills.Offer mindfulness and stress management techniques.Encourage physical health through exercise and nutrition education.Foster community interaction to reduce social isolation.MethodologyOur Foundation has enlisted experts in psychology, physical health, and education to create a comprehensive curriculum. Modules will be delivered via workshops, online resources, and one-to-one mentoring. Special attention will be given to adapt the programme to local cultures and individual needs.Impact AssessmentMetrics of success will include not only skill acquisition but also improvement in mental wellbeing, demonstrated by periodic surveys and interviews. Data will be meticulously analysed to refine the programme for greater efficacy and broader reach.SustainabilityIn line with CNCF's long-term vision, the Noble Academy of Wellbeing and Happiness aims to become a self-sustaining social enterprise, with revenue generated from services offered to the public channelled back into expanding the programme.This initiative represents a pivotal step in CNCF's ongoing efforts to uplift communities not just through material aid but by fortifying emotional and mental resilience.", "start_date": "Jan 2023", "title": "Noble Academy of Wellbeing and Happiness"}, {"description": "The development objective of the Mekong Delta Water Resources Management for Rural Development Project is to protect and enhance the utilization of water resources in the project provinces of the Mekong Delta Region in order to sustain gains in agricultural productivity, provide access to water supply for rural households, and contribute to climate change adaptation. The project consists of the following components: 1) water management planning and efficient utilization, including measures to strengthen institutional capacities at sub-regional and provincial levels, promote effective operations and maintenance, and promote on-farm water use efficiency through pilot schemes; 2) improvement and rehabilitation of water resources infrastructure, with emphasis on rehabilitation and minor improvement through canal dredging, and embankment reinforcement, as well as the installation of secondary control sluice gates; 3) rural water supply and sanitation, aiming to extend reliable services to about 60,000 households in the project area; and 4) project management and implementation support, supporting incremental operating costs to implement the project and the monitoring and evaluation of project outcomes and impacts.", "end_date": "Sep 2017", "start_date": "Jun 2011", "title": "Vietnam Mekong Delta Water Resources Management for Rural Development Project - (MDWRM-RDP)"}, {"description": "This research project investigates the impact of remittances from Ho Chi Minh City migrants on the living standards of their dependents in An Giang province. Utilising a snowball sampling technique, the study surveys 107 respondents across four An Giang districts and 40 migrants in Ho Chi Minh City. Focused on less privileged households, the project explores the ramifications of migration and remittances on child well-being.Our findings confirm the importance of remittances for improving living standards but highlight their inadequacy for providing comprehensive care, especially in education and healthcare. Remittance consistency is jeopardised when migrants lack stable employment, thus impacting the care received by left-behind children. Moreover, elderly caregivers in An Giang require more than financial aid; they need know-how in effective healthcare spending and educational nurturing.The study also suggests supplementary support methods for caregivers, such as creating job opportunities and facilitating easier access to financial tools for irregular remittance inflows. While the research is constrained by its small, purpose-focused sample from impoverished Mekong Delta areas, it contributes to our evolving understanding of how migration dynamics affect Vietnam's rural youth.This study offers a nuanced perspective, adding layers to CNCF's existing initiatives aimed at sustainable upliftment of disadvantaged communities.", "end_date": "Apr 2012", "start_date": "Aug 2011", "title": "Research Project on the Migration to Ho Chi Minh City and the Impacts of Remittances on the Left-Behind Children in An Giang Province"}, {"description": "Enhancing Data Quality in Vietnam's Household SurveysBackground:Hired by the World Bank DC as a consultant, Nha Nguyen leads a project aimed at optimising the quality of the Vietnam Household Living Standard Survey (VHLSS) 2010 data. The World Bank, in collaboration with Vietnam's General Statistics Office, has been conducting Living Standard Measurement Study (LSMS) surveys in Vietnam since 1992. However, there is a critical gap in the survey's data quality and operational efficiency.Objectives:The project seeks to re-interview a sub-sample of the VHLSS 2010 dataset across 20 selected Vietnamese provinces. Information from these re-interviews will be utilised to evaluate both the survey's quality and the effectiveness of a newly established monitoring team.Key Tasks:Assemble a team of 20 re-interviewers.Coordinate training sessions for re-interviewers in Ho Chi Minh City.Facilitate the re-interview process, lasting for a period of six weeks.Supply a dedicated office space, including essential calling equipment.Execute field visits, and data entry for re-interview results.Expected Output:A comprehensive STATA dataset and a detailed implementation report are to be submitted by May 2011, adhering to World Bank quality standards.Quality and Experience:Nha Nguyen brings his wealth of experience in project management, particularly in data-driven projects focused on societal upliftment, making him well-suited for ensuring the survey meets high-quality benchmarks.This initiative aligns with Nha's ongoing commitment to evidence-based policy-making and social impact, providing crucial data that can inform future sustainable development projects.", "end_date": "May 2011", "start_date": "Oct 2010", "title": "Implementation of re-interviewing a sub-sample of Vietnam Household Living Standard Survey 2010 under the Project Improving Quality of Survey Data"}, {"description": "Research on Antibodies Circulating Influenza A/H5N1 Virus in Humans: Ensuring Quality Data Collection and Effective CommunicationRole: Field Operations SupervisorDuration: July 2010 - September 2010Locations: Thua Thien Hue, Tien Giang, VietnamAffiliations: The Henry M. Jackson Foundation, World Bank, General Statistics Office of Vietnam, National Institute of Hygiene and EpidemiologyBackgroundAmid growing concerns about the spread of Influenza A/H5N1, this collaborative research initiative aims to understand the rate of antibodies circulating the virus in humans in two key provinces of Vietnam. Accurate, reliable data is critical for formulating effective public health policies and interventions.ObjectivesAs Field Operations Supervisor, Nha Nguyen was responsible for:Ensuring data quality through rigorous supervision of field operations.Facilitating effective communication between local communities and research institutions.MethodologyThe project deployed cutting-edge epidemiological techniques, combined with robust data verification protocols. Culturally sensitive communication strategies were employed to educate and gain the trust of local participants.OutcomesThe project resulted in high-quality datasets that contributed to Vietnam\u2019s preparedness against Influenza A/H5N1. It also enhanced local understanding of the virus, thereby fostering community resilience against future outbreaks.Through stringent data collection processes and effective community engagement, the project exemplifies a balanced blend of technical rigor and social sensitivity. name: Tai Tuan Phan
about: With over 15 years of IT experience managing brand expansion globally, I have worked in Europe, the USA, and Brazil for brands such as Seagate, DXC and FEC. I specialize in Enterprise Applications and Digital Consumer Products relate BFSI domain. I started my professional career in my late 20s being employed by several firms in Vietnam as Software Developer, Business Analyst, IT Manager, and IT Delivery. Capabilities: As Software Delivery Manager, I build, supervise, and enhance every stage of the digital product life cycle, having expertise in a variety of specialties: System Analysis, Modeling, UX/UI prototyping, Technical Writing, Coding and Testing, Security and System Administration, Software/Hardware Development Process, BPM, Scrum, Agile Process. As IT Manager, I have a knowledge background in IT Governance Frameworks, Enterprise Architecture, Strategic Planning, Compliance, and Due Diligence. As Entrepreneur, business development. I lead a small team to have a pitch deck and work on both products and finding customers to outsource software development to fund products. As Team Lead, I focus on providing Mission and Leadership to my team, I practice fluent communications and mutual challenge within the team. Specialties: * Software/Hardware Development Process * Agile/Scrum Development Methodology * Product Development * Change Management * Customer and Crisis Management * Strategic and Operational Planning * Quality, Performance, and Process Improvement * Enterprise Architecture * Development and Engineering Services * External Partners Management * IT Governance Frameworks * Value Engineering * Strategic Planning * Compliance * Due Diligence * Business Development

name: Minh Tuan H.
about: Giao tiếp tốt 4 kỹ năng tiếng Anh và thuần thục tiếng Việt và kỹ năng làm việc ở ngành nghề dịch vụ tư vấn, thuyết trình, kinh doanh và bán hàng. ■ ĐỊNH HƯỚNG NGHỀ NGHIỆP: - Tập trung phát triển chuyên sâu nghề chăm sóc khách hàng quốc tế (tiếng Anh) và kinh doanh, quan hệ khách hàng. - Phát huy thế mạnh ở tiếng Anh và dịch vụ, kinh doanh bán hàng đóng góp cho hoạt động của công ty. ■ KINH NGHIỆM LÀM VIỆC: - Có 5 năm kinh nghiệm trong thuyết trình tư vấn khách hàng; sales marketing trong các lĩnh vực: 1) bán hàng và dịch vụ, 2) giáo dục quốc tế và 3) FMCG - Hỗ trợ quản lý và đồng nghiệp hiệu quả. Tính cách hoà đồng, học hỏi, khiêm tốn, và biết lắng nghe, chia sẻ. - Có những sản phẩm thuyết trình sáng tạo được các công ty lưu giữ. Có kinh nghiệm làm việc ở các môi trường khác nhau ở Việt Nam và nước ngoài. ————————— ■ KỸ NĂNG NGHỀ NGHIỆP: - Khả năng thuyết trình sản phẩm kinh doanh, xử lý tình huống với khách hàng, khả năng chăm sóc khách hàng tận tâm, sáng tạo. - Giao tiếp tiếng Việt, tiếng Anh thông thạo, rõ ràng và chuyên nghiệp. Viết và trình bày ngôn ngữ tốt. - Có khả năng phân tích tốt, cẩn thận với các chi tiết, có khả năng lắng nghe, tiếp thu và xử lý nhu cầu các bên công bằng, hợp lý. - Luôn mong muốn phục vụ khách hàng, tạo trải nghiệm tốt đẹp cho khách hàng và nhãn hàng KD. Đem lại lợi ích cho công ty, khách hàng, các đối tác. ————————— ■ CHỨNG CHỈ, CHỨNG NHẬN: - Khen thưởng về chất lượng chăm sóc khách hàng (sinh viên) tốt, chủ động và sáng tạo của Trưởng Bộ phận dịch vụ sinh viên trường ĐH Nam Úc (2017). - Đại diện HSV Vietnam ở Nam Úc thuyết trình ở sự kiện giao lưu giữa các đại diện tư vấn viên du học các nước và StudyAdelaide (2016) ở Adelaide Town Hall - Toà thị chính Nam Úc. - Danh hiệu SV lãnh đạo trẻ, đóng góp cho cộng đồng (Rotary Youth Leadership Award) at Nunyara Conference Center. - Có mặt trong các video, brochure, trang web quảng cáo về trường, công ty, và dịch vụ của tất cả các trường đã cộng tác: Eynesbury, University of South Australia, Kaplan Business School, IDP Education Vietnam. - SV lãnh đạo trẻ tiêu biểu (International Marketing Activites) của trường ĐH Nam Úc, giai đoạn 2017-2018. - Giải Nhì Cuộc Thi Hùng Biện Tiếng Anh - Khoa Anh - ĐH Sư Phạm TP.HCM năm 2015. ■ PROJECT: - His 2019 interview video project in collaboration with IDP Education (top market leader Australia and Vietnam-wide) can be accessed here: https://fb.watch/bkBdvgSuQB/ https://www.facebook.com/idpvietnam/videos/464666177462437?sfns=mo - 2nd and 3rd video project (agency) https://fb.watch/6AQbgpWd0a/ https://fb.watch/v/35InA1Pi2/

recommendations: ["Abigail C. “I came to know Thomas during my time in AIESEC in South Australia. Although I did not work with Thomas directly, I interacted with him numerous times on a personal level. He has a strong drive to succeed and demonstrates enthusiasm in his work through his determination of accomplishing what he sets to achieve. Thomas is a natural communicator and has a wonderful and hilarious sense of humour, making it a pleasure to work with him. He exudes great confidence but is an approachable and down-to-earth person. He also exhibits a growth mindset and is consistently taking advantage of the opportunities presented to him to develop himself personally and professionally. Not only is he proactive but is also a helpful person and an effective team player. It is without any reservation that I recommend Thomas as a highly capable individual who would be a valuable asset to any future organisation he may be involved with.”","James Young “Thomas takes initiative and an all-round a proactive person. He was the person who introduced me to Rotary Norwood Club. His generosity in helping people leads him to write a meticulous email to Rotary recommending me to the club. Starting from that point onwards, we volunteer together and I could see more of his enthusiasm on giving back to society. It was from Thomas, I learnt the happiest people are not those getting more, but those giving more.”"]

projects: [{"description": "North Kuwait Asset is managing the production from two oil field giants and two oil field minors, namely Raudatain, Sabriah, Ratqa and Abdali, respectively.In 2007/08, a long term strategy to increase production from North Kuwait to 1 MMBOPD by 2016/17 was established. To achieve that, a development plan was put in place to reach the target by 2016/17. The plan aimed to achieve the aforementioned objective through, extensive infill drilling, pressure maintenance and increasing facility capacity with new gathering centres Based on the 2013/14 AAP, the 1 MMBOPD target has been shifted to 2017/18 and sustain plateau for fifteen years before the onset of production decline and to align with delivery of critical projects, namely:\u2022Drilling and completion of 376 new wells from 2013/14 through 2017/18\u2022Pressure maintenance projects including Effluent water injection Phase-1 and Central Sea Water injection Phase-2\u20223 new Gathering Centers with associated headers, manifolds and trunk lines\u2022Power supply substation installations\u2022Provision of Consulting support and Manpower requirements", "end_date": "May 2016", "start_date": "Dec 2012", "title": "North Kuwait deep drilling"}, {"description": "PTTEP & Partners discover 2 oil fields in Algeria21 Sep 12.Mr. Tevin Vongvanich, President and CEO of PTT Exploration and Production Public Company Limited or PTTEP disclosed that PTTEP with its Partners SONATRACH and CNOOC Limited has successfully discovered crude oil in the 4th and the 5th exploration wells in Hassi Bir Rekaiz project, Algeria.The 4th exploration well (OGB-1) was drilled and yielded petroleum discovery in formation of Ordovician (Ouargla Sandstone) reservoir. The flow tests (Drill Stem Test - DST) were conducted and discovered the crude oil flow at approximately 485 barrels per day (BPD). Triassic Argilo Greseux Inf\u00e9rieur (TAG-I) reservoir which is the same formation with oil occurrence in the first well (RTF-1) in early 2012 exhibits oil flows as well but amount need to assess further. The joint venture is considering further appraisal program for reserve assessment.For the 5th exploration well (BOG-1), it was drilled and yielded petroleum discovery in the same 2 petroleum bearing formations of Ordovician (Ouargla Sandstone) reservoir and TAG-I reservoir. According to the DST flow test, the crude oil flow at approximately 553 BPD and 1,870 BPD, respectively.It is important to note that the discovery of Ordovician (Ouargla Sandstone) reservoir in Hassi Bir Rekaiz project is considered to be another success of PTTEP in Algeria.PTTEP and its partners have planned in the 1st exploration phase of Hassi Bir Rekaiz project to drill 9 exploration wells during late 2011 to early 2013. Hassi Bir Rekaiz project is located onshore in the Eastern part of Algeria. It covers an area of 5,378 square kilometers. Apart from Hassi Bir Rekaiz, PTTEP also jointly operates the Bir Seba field, in Algeria 433a & 416b project in Algeria, which is currently under the development phase.For more information, please contact Corporate E-mail: bussaban@pttep.comE-mail: tongchitp@pttep.comE-mail: pirayas@pttep.com", "end_date": "Nov 2009", "start_date": "Mar 2006", "title": "PVEP Algieria"}]
